# TESS Program G08158

Title: The Search For Star-Planet Interactions With TESS

PI: Rapetti, David - Universities Space Research Association (Usra)

Type: LARGE

Summary: Magnetic fields are key to understanding planetary interiors, atmospheric escape, and habitability, yet exoplanet magnetic fields remain undetected. Hot Jupiter magnetic fields can be probed via magnetic star-planet interactions (SPI) that induce phase-dependent stellar flares. TESS data has shown tentative SPI flaring on the hot Jupiter HIP 67522 b. We propose to search for SPI flares in Cycle 8 data and analyze all exoplanet systems (including HIP 67522 and hundreds of hot Jupiters) and thousands of flare stars using new systematic-correction tools. Confirmed SPI flares would reveal exoplanet magnetic fields; statistically significant non-detections will help guide future searches. We will also provide community tools and data products with wide applications to other science cases.


# Sector 097 targets observed (2 min: 1572, 20 s: 118)
